scope: Evidence-grade tables for the first three demonstrator use cases in LiverTx-OmicsDB.

evidence_grade_definition.A: Reusable sample-level matrix with explicit per-sample labels.
evidence_grade_definition.B: Reusable feature-level table with explicit group contrasts.
evidence_grade_definition.C: Marker-layer evidence recovered from article figures, partial tables, or group-summary biomarker reports.
evidence_grade_definition.R: Reference context layer used for interpretation, not direct transplant outcome claims.
evidence_grade_definition.M: Metadata-model or unresolved candidate layer; useful for planning but not yet a user-facing evidence layer.

demonstrator_use_cases.title: Early injury versus rejection
demonstrator_use_cases.clinical_question: Can public molecular evidence distinguish early graft injury or ischemia/reperfusion-linked dysfunction from true immune rejection?

records.main_caveat: This is the strongest current tissue-level anchor, but same-study marker evidence is still exploratory until independently reproduced.
Direct graft-tissue proteomics is valuable for injury context, but this is an early-AKI proxy layer rather than a direct injury-versus-rejection classifier.
This is published group-summary serum biomarker evidence, not a reusable per-sample transplant proteome matrix.
This is a small six-sample discovery-set proteomics layer and should remain marker-level evidence until independently replicated.
This layer supports liver-cell interpretation only and must not be treated as direct transplant injury or rejection evidence.

demonstrator_use_cases.title: Donor liver quality and graft viability
demonstrator_use_cases.clinical_question: Can donor-organ molecular features contextualize accepted versus rejected livers and machine-perfusion viability biology?

records.main_caveat: Accepted versus rejected labels describe donor-organ selection status, not post-transplant recipient outcomes.
This is donor-organ viability biology during machine perfusion, not a complete recipient-outcome layer.

records.contrast: B1_vs_B2_vs_B3 sample mapping recovered; biliary-complication outcome annotation unresolved
records.main_caveat: Sample-to-timepoint mapping is public, but per-graft complication labels and a lightweight reusable protein matrix still need recovery before this can be promoted.

demonstrator_use_cases.title: Blood-based monitoring and tolerance context
demonstrator_use_cases.clinical_question: Can blood, PBMC, plasma, serum, metabolite, and proteomic evidence support non-invasive monitoring and tolerance-related stratification?

records.main_caveat: This layer currently supports longitudinal blood monitoring, not a biopsy-validated rejection-outcome classifier.
This is a tolerance-focused PBMC layer and should not be collapsed into generic rejection-monitoring claims.
This is a strong serum metabolomics layer, but independent validation is still needed before claiming non-invasive classification performance.
This is post-transplant renal-dysfunction monitoring evidence, not rejection-specific blood proteomics.
This is currently an article-figure marker panel while the unresolved DataSheet1 supplement blocks full-table promotion.
This is partial peri-transplant serum context rather than a complete longitudinal sample-level proteomics matrix.
This is useful serum biomarker context, but it remains group-summary evidence rather than a reusable sample-level transplant proteome matrix.
